headline: Earthquakes
description: Earthquakes occur when tectonic plates suddenly slip along faults, releasing accumulated stress as seismic waves. Measured by seismographs. Magnitude scales: Richter (local, historical), Moment Magnitude (Mw, modern — logarithmic: each 1.0 increase = 32x more energy). Largest recorded: 1960 Chile (Mw 9.5).
